creator: alex@big.endian.de
text:
Thus spake Yin-Jieh Chen (yinjieh@Crazyman.Dorm13.NCTU.edu.tw):

> +	@${RM} -f ${PREFIX}/share/vim/${DISTNAME:S/-//:S/.//}/gvimrc_example.vim
> +	@${RM} -f ${PREFIX}/share/vim/${DISTNAME:S/-//:S/.//}/tools/gvimdiff

What's wrong with having an example gvimrc?
And why is gvimdiff installed then? What is it? A tools seems fine.

However, Why don't you just add them to PLIST as well?

Alex

-- 
cat: /home/alex/.sig: No such file or directory
